name: Frontify Brand Management Platform API
what_it_does: Brand management and design system platform with GraphQL API — enabling marketing and design teams to manage brand guidelines, digital assets (DAM), design system documentation, and branded templates in a unified brand portal, with customizable brand guidelines sites, asset libraries, and team collaboration for brand consistency across all touchpoints.
use_cases: Querying brand assets and guidelines programmatically via Frontify GraphQL API for marketing automation
Managing digital assets and media files in Frontify DAM via API for content pipeline integration
Creating and updating brand guideline pages via Frontify API for automated documentation publishing
Integrating Frontify brand assets with CMS and DAM systems via API for content production
Querying design system component status and documentation via Frontify API for governance reporting
not_for: Design file creation (use Figma for design work — Frontify is for brand management and documentation)
Component development and testing (use Storybook for component development)
Project management (use Jira or Linear for issue tracking)
best_when: An enterprise marketing team or large design organization needs a branded, client-facing brand portal with digital asset management, design system documentation, and templated content creation — ensuring brand consistency across agencies, partners, and global teams.
avoid_when: You don't have a formal brand system, are a small team where Notion suffices, primarily need design file collaboration (not brand management), or need deep code component integration.

auth.notes: OAuth2 authorization code and client credentials flows. Bearer token auth. Workspace-scoped with fine-grained resource permissions. Webhook for brand asset, guideline, and template change events. GraphQL API (not REST) for all operations. JavaScript/TypeScript SDK available. Scoped by resource type (assets, guidelines, templates, users). SSO via SAML for enterprise. Brand App SDK for custom Frontify extensions.

pricing.paid_tiers: Professional ~$79/user/month; Enterprise custom; contact sales for pricing
pricing.notes: Swiss company (St. Gallen). Founded 2013. Raised $50M Series B (2021). Backed by Bain Capital Ventures, Espira Investments. 3,000+ enterprise customers. Customers include Lufthansa, LIDL, Salesforce, Uber. Strong in European enterprise market. Brand guidelines portal as key differentiator — custom branded URL for external sharing. Digital asset management + brand guidelines + design system in one platform. GDPR-first as Swiss company.

performance.rate_limits: Enterprise rate limits per workspace; contact sales for specifics

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: GraphQL API (not REST) for all operations. OAuth2 auth with scopes. Good developer documentation. JavaScript/TypeScript SDK available. Webhook for asset and guideline change events. Brand App SDK for custom extensions. GraphQL requires different query patterns than REST — use schema introspection for discovery. Swiss company with GDPR-first design.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Asset queries fully idempotent. Guideline page reads idempotent. GraphQL queries idempotent. Mutations require careful idempotency handling — check resource state before mutation.
known_agent_gotchas: GraphQL API (not REST) — agents must use GraphQL queries/mutations, not standard REST patterns
Annual contract minimum — significant commitment before seeing production value
Enterprise pricing (~$79+/user/month) makes it expensive for smaller teams
Brand App SDK for customization requires JavaScript/TypeScript development
OAuth2 scopes per resource type — need to configure correct scopes for each use case

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. GDPR, SOC2, ISO27001 compliant. EU and US data residency. OAuth2 with fine-grained scopes. Swiss company with strong privacy standards. Brand assets may include unreleased campaigns and confidential designs — strict access control required. SAML SSO for enterprise identity management. Brand App SDK runs in Frontify sandbox — review custom extension security.
